title: Asian stocks are mixed after big tech sell-off
description: Asian stocks are mixed on Wednesday, following a sell-off in big technology stocks from Asia to Wall Street. U.S. stock futures are also trading mixed. Global investors are monitoring market movements in Japan and South Korea, which have seen big gains in recent months on the global AI boom but both fell sharply on Tuesday. South Korea's benchmark Kospi index was up 0.5% on Wednesday, while Japan's Nikkei 225 lost 1.1%. On Wall Street, the benchmark S&P 500 fell 1.4% Tuesday. The tech-heavy Nasdaq composite dropped 2.2%, while the Dow was 0.1% lower. Oil prices fell as more ships crossed the Strait of Hormuz and as U.S.-Iran talks on a permanent end to the Iran war progressed.

title: Nithin Kamath recalls falling for a pyramid scheme, warns retail investors against 'easy money' promises
description: Zerodha co-founder Nithin Kamath shared his past entanglement with a pyramid scheme in his late teens, emphasizing that wealth building requires no shortcuts. He warned against schemes promising unusually high returns, noting that many continue to operate in India despite growing financial awareness. Kamath cautioned that making money in the stock market isn't easy and urged people to avoid referral-based money-making schemes, calling them almost invariably fraudulent.

title: Germany's Frigate Shift: A New Course in Naval Defense
description: Germany opted to cancel its F126 frigate program due to cost overruns and delays, impacting Rheinmetall's stock. The project has been replaced with eight smaller Meko A-200 frigates from Thyssenkrupp, boosting TKMS's market value. This decision brings significant implications for Europe's defence industry landscape.
